relativization.citation: Baker T., Gill J., Solovay R. (1975). Relativizations of the P=?NP question. SIAM J. Comput. 4(4):431-442.

natural_proofs.citation: Razborov A., Rudich S. (1997). Natural proofs. J. Comput. Syst. Sci. 55(1):24-35.

three_properties.largeness: The combinatorial property Φ used in the lower bound holds for a non-negligible fraction (>= 2^{-O(n)}) of all Boolean functions on n bits.
three_properties.constructivity: Given the truth-table of f (size 2^n), membership f in Φ is decidable in time polynomial in 2^n, i.e., P/poly-uniform in the truth-table.
three_properties.usefulness: If f in Φ, then f has no small circuits (i.e., f not in the targeted complexity class C).

natural_proofs.rr_theorem: If strong pseudorandom functions exist in C (e.g., sub-exponential hardness of discrete log / factoring => PRFs in TC^0), then no natural property useful against C can exist.

algebrization.citation: Aaronson S., Wigderson A. (2009). Algebrization: A new barrier in complexity theory. ACM Trans. Comput. Theory 1(1):2:1-2:54.

gct_obstruction_insufficiency.citation: Ikenmeyer C., Panova G. (2017). Rectangular Kronecker coefficients and plethysms in geometric complexity theory. Advances in Mathematics 319:40-66.

bypass_rationale.text: An MCSP-based reduction argues that the Minimum Circuit Size Problem (MCSP) is NP-hard, from which super-polynomial circuit lower bounds follow under standard derandomization hypotheses. The reduction does not, at any step, give a polynomial-time (in truth-table size) predicate that isolates hard functions by inspecting the truth-table; it only uses the NP-hardness of a decision problem over circuit-size descriptions. Consequently, the combinatorial property implicit in the lower bound is not constructive in the Razborov-Rudich sense, which breaks one of the three conjunctive requirements of a natural proof.

literature_refs.citation: Hirahara S. (2018). Non-Black-Box Worst-Case to Average-Case Reductions within NP. 59th FOCS, pp. 247-258.
Hirahara S. (2022). NP-Hardness of Learning Programs and Partial MCSP. 63rd FOCS, pp. 968-979. (Best Paper)
Allender E., Hirahara S. (2019). New Insights on the (Non-)Hardness of Circuit Minimization and Related Problems. ACM Trans. Comput. Theory 11(4):27:1-27:27.
Kabanets V., Cai J.-Y. (2000). Circuit minimization problem. Proc. 32nd STOC, pp. 73-79.
Impagliazzo R., Wigderson A. (1997). P = BPP if E requires exponential circuits: Derandomizing the XOR lemma. Proc. 29th STOC, pp. 220-229.
Williams R. (2011). Non-uniform ACC circuit lower bounds. J. ACM 61(1):2:1-2:32. (NEXP not-subset ACC^0, algorithmic method)

steps: S1 (LITERATURE): Fix MCSP = { (x, s) : x is a truth-table of a Boolean function with circuit complexity <= s }. Kabanets-Cai (2000) prove MCSP in NP; its exact complexity is open.

S2 (LITERATURE): Hirahara (2022, FOCS) proves Partial-MCSP is NP-hard under randomized polynomial-time reductions. The reduction uses a pseudo-random generator construction conditioned on E requiring 2^{Omega(n)} circuits (Impagliazzo-Wigderson 1997).
S3 (CONJECTURE): Strengthen Partial-MCSP NP-hardness to full-MCSP NP-hardness under deterministic polynomial-time reductions. This step is OPEN and is the principal gap of the strategy.

S4 (DERIVED, conditional): Given S3 and P = BPP (implied by E >= 2^{Omega(n)} circuits under Impagliazzo-Wigderson), one derives that E requires super-polynomial circuits, hence P != NP. Because the derivation is through an NP-hardness reduction on circuit-size descriptions rather than through a truth-table-constructive predicate, it does not satisfy Razborov-Rudich constructivity.

S5 (PENDING): Verify that the resulting proof also evades relativization (MCSP-style reductions are plausibly non-relativizing per Allender-Hirahara 2019) and algebrization (this is currently unverified and would require a dedicated argument).

known_obstructions.text: Full-MCSP NP-hardness is OPEN. All positive NP-hardness results cover Partial-MCSP, MKtP (Kolmogorov variants), Minimum-KT-Complexity, or restricted models; full-MCSP under deterministic poly-time reductions remains unresolved (Hirahara survey 2023).
Hirahara's Partial-MCSP reduction is randomized (RP) and depends on derandomization hypotheses (E requires 2^{Omega(n)} circuits). Removing the randomness or the hypothesis is open.
Allender-Hirahara (2019) and related work show that many standard reduction toolkits yield only magnification-style conditional consequences, not unconditional super-polynomial lower bounds.

Evading constructivity does not automatically evade relativization or algebrization; a full non-naturalizing proof must check all three barriers. The current sketch only addresses constructivity explicitly.

verifiable_sub_goals.text: Re-verify Hirahara-2022 Partial-MCSP NP-hardness statement from the published proof and record the exact reduction size parameters (for atlas cross-reference).

Identify one concrete restricted-circuit class C (e.g., DNF, depth-2 threshold) for which MCSP[C] is provably NP-hard under deterministic reductions and check whether the proof is already known to be non-natural against C.
Construct an explicit example showing that the Razborov-Rudich constructivity predicate CANNOT be extracted from Hirahara's Partial-MCSP reduction (formal non-naturalness certificate).

Attempt a weaker conditional target: super-polynomial lower bound against a sub-class of P/poly (e.g., formulas of size n^{1+eps}) via the metacomplexity route, to produce an intermediate benchmark result.

counter_examples.title: GCT program stalled at occurrence obstructions
counter_examples.citation: Ikenmeyer C., Panova G. (2017). Rectangular Kronecker coefficients and plethysms in geometric complexity theory. Advances in Mathematics 319:40-66.
counter_examples.text: Mulmuley's Geometric Complexity Theory (GCT) aimed to separate permanent from determinant via representation-theoretic obstructions (thus non-natural). Ikenmeyer-Panova 2017 proved that occurrence obstructions, the specific device GCT had relied on, are insufficient to separate VP from VNP; thicker obstructions are required and none are known. A non-natural program alone does not imply progress.

counter_examples.title: Williams 2011 NEXP not-subset ACC^0 is non-natural but terminates far from NP
counter_examples.citation: Williams R. (2011). Non-uniform ACC circuit lower bounds. J. ACM 61(1):2:1-2:32.
counter_examples.text: Williams's algorithmic method produced a non-naturalizing proof that NEXP is not a subset of ACC^0. However the class ACC^0 is extraordinarily weak relative to P/poly, and subsequent extensions (ACC^0 to TC^0, NQP lower bounds) have not reached anywhere near NP-vs-P. Non-naturalness is a necessary but not sufficient condition for P != NP.

counter_examples.title: Algebrization blocks most circuit lower bound techniques
counter_examples.citation: Aaronson S., Wigderson A. (2009). Algebrization: A new barrier in complexity theory. ACM Trans. Comput. Theory 1(1):2:1-2:54.
counter_examples.text: Aaronson-Wigderson show that any technique respecting algebraic extensions of oracles cannot settle P vs NP. Arithmetization-based natural proofs, interactive proof techniques, and many standard circuit methods algebrize. Evading naturalness (constructivity) does not by itself evade algebrization; a bypass strategy must be checked against all three barriers (relativization, naturalization, algebrization).

honesty_statement.text: This artifact is a strategy proposal, not a proof. No step constitutes a bypass of the Razborov-Rudich barrier; the metacomplexity route is identified as the most promising non-natural direction in the current literature, with the central open step (full-MCSP NP-hardness under deterministic reductions) remaining OPEN. BT-542 resolution status is 0/1 (unsolved). n=6 structural prior is NOT applicable to any step of this sketch (third reconfirmation, cross-ref BARRIER-PX-1, BYPASS-PX-1 in atlas.n6).

n6_applicability.text: sigma(6)=12, tau(6)=4, phi(6)=2, sopfr(6)=5 have no identified connection to MCSP circuit-size parameters, Hirahara reduction padding, or Impagliazzo-Wigderson derandomization constants. BT-542 is structurally independent of CANON.
